item.title: How adoptees from Bangladesh search for their past | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
From the late 1960s onwards, thousands of Bangladeshi children were adopted abroad in the hope of a better future. But behind this humanitarian façade lay a web of trafficking, deception, and lost identities. DW uncovers the heartbreaking truth of stolen pasts and follows two adoptees living in the Netherlands as they search for their birth families.
00:00 Intro 01:30 Meet Sander, adoptee from Bangladesh 05:38 How the Shapla Community is helping adoptees 06:58 Suma’s search for her birth family 10:42 Sander meets his biological family 17:36 Context and history: Overseas adoptions 25:50 Situation of children in Bangladesh today 27:05 Life as an adoptee vs. in the birth family
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #dwstories ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. Meet intriguing people, travel to distant lands, get a look behind the complexities of daily life and build a deeper understanding of current affairs and global events. Subscribe and explore the world around you with DW Documentary.

item.title: Remembering Van Cliburn at the 1958 Tchaikovsky Competition
item.author: Ben Laude
item.content:encoded:
View the 1958 Tchaikovsky Competition Program: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1T4N_owgwCweTa0k9Jpvsm6aBnKwr0q37/view?usp=sharing
Listen to Van Cliburn's complete Tchaikovsky Competition performances: https://open.spotify.com/album/1WYPS3sK8IlodeVzqAcFrd
0:00 Jerry's non-socialist program 4:51 Van Cliburn's program 6:21 Going to the Soviet Union was like going to the moon 10:17 Stories about Vladimir Ashkenazy, Lazar Berman, and their future wives 16:04 Meeting Nikita Khrushchev and Heinrich Neuhaus 18:35 Hearing Sviatoslav Richter and playing Scriabin at Scriabin's home 24:47 Misbehaving in Moscow 27:16 Van Cliburn, diplomat 33:30 Other competitors: Naum Shtarkman, Lev Vlassenko, Daniel Pollack 37:41 Contrasting Cliburn's playing with the Russians' 39:29 How Cliburn got back at the world 42:31 Did Cliburn get too famous? 44:40 Final reflections on Van and the Competition
Thank you to Leora Lowenthal for helping arrange this interview, and to Jerry Lowenthal for sharing his memories with the world.
--
https://www.instagram.com/benlawdy/ https://patreon.com/BenLaude https://benlaude.com/

item.title: Timeline TV: 1999 - Everything That Happened In TV In The Year 1999
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
In the remaining year of the 90s, all sorts of things were happening on Television! 1999 was the year Prince had been waiting for since 1982, and it did not disappoint. Several classic shows debuted right at the buzzer of the new millennium, including one that would change the face of television dramas for the next three decades.. Tell us what you were watching on TV in 1999? Were YOU afraid of the upcoming Y2K threat?
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#timeline #1999 #weirdhistory

item.title: Meet The Torvosaurus: Europe's Apex Carnivore | Walking with Dinosaurs | BBC Earth Science
item.author: BBC Earth Science
item.content:encoded:
The terrifying Torvosaurus could rip its prey to shreds, but the giant Lusotitan was definitely off the menu. Or so it was thought, until an expert noted something peculiar…
Best of Earth Science: http://bit.ly/EarthLabOriginals Best of BBC Earth: http://bit.ly/TheBestOfBBCEarthVideos
Taken from 'Walking with Dinosaurs' (2025) Six dig sites. Six stories. Six prehistoric heroes. Discover the incredible lives of dinosaurs unearthed from real-world excavations in a reimagining of the iconic series. Pioneering special effects recreate the twists and turns of the age of the dinosaurs in character-led stories with a truly heavyweight cast.
This is a channel from BBC Studios who help fund new BBC programmes. Service information and feedback: http://bbcworldwide.com/vod-feedback--contact-details.aspx

item.title: Icicle: The Highs & Lows of Optimizing DSLs • Jacob Stanley • YOW! 2016
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at YOW! 2016. #GOTOcon #YOW https://yowcon.com
Jacob Stanley - Software Engineer at Ambiata
RESOURCES https://github.com/jacobstanley https://x.com/jacobstanley https://github.com/icicle-lang/icicle-ambiata
ABSTRACT So, you’ve written a #DSL, it’s a work of art! It so perfectly models the problem at hand that even a trained house cat could generate business value. You just have one problem, your interpreter runs 100x slower than a shell script launching JVMs in a loop.
#DomainSpecificLanguages (DSLs) are able to offer the abstraction benefits of a high-level language, while when compiled, competing with low-level languages on performance. Because DSLs have a much smaller problem area, many specialised optimisations can be performed which are not feasible for a general purpose language.
In this talk, we will explain a number of optimisations which are useful for DSLs, and show our solutions to the practical issues we faced when implementing them in our #QueryLanguage, #Icicle.
Icicle is a query language for processing large amounts of time-series data. Its type system enforces that queries require only a single pass over the data, and that multiple queries over the same data can be fused together. By using a combination of high level optimisations such as partial evaluation and common subexpression elimination, and low level optimisations such as parsing code which is specialised for the given input, our queries are faster than the equivalent hand-written C code.
From this talk, the audience will learn how the focused point of view that DSLs provide can be useful in performance critical systems, as well as a number of concrete techniques for optimising their own DSLs.
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Kenneth E. Iverson • A Programming Language • https://amzn.to/45sCYKK Hadley Wickham • Advanced R • https://amzn.to/45XIbKw Jeffry A. Borror • q For Mortals Version • https://amzn.to/3zd58eo Nick Psaris • Q Tips • https://amzn.to/4cxJr76 Nazia Habib • Hands-On Q-Learning with Python • https://amzn.to/3zfUbZM
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://twitter.com/GOTOcon https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #FunctionalProgramming #SoftwareEngineering #Programmer #JacobStanley
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join
Looking for a unique learning experience? Attend the next GOTO conference near you! Get your ticket at https://gotopia.tech Sign up for updates and specials at https://gotopia.tech/newsletter
SUBSCRIBE TO OUR CHANNEL - new videos posted almost daily. https://www.youtube.com/user/GotoConferences/?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: Main challenges living on a reservation
item.author: Channel 5 with Andrew Callaghan

item.title: Iga Ninja: The Truth Behind The Shadow Warriors Of Feudal Japan
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
This documentary uncovers the incredible true story of the Iga Ninja, the legendary shadow warriors of feudal Japan. Often seen as mythical figures, these elite special forces were masters of deception, espionage, and unconventional warfare. From their brutal training in the Iga mountains to their daring missions of assassination and infiltration, learn how the ninja became an indispensable, yet feared, part of Japan's military machine, shaping the course of its history.
Do you love uncovering the past, exploring historic sites, and venturing to distant lands? Join History Hit today and stream hundreds of exclusive documentaries, with new releases every week. Plus, enjoy ad-free access to our podcast network for even more history every week.
Head over to https://eu1.hubs.ly/H0gFQRz0 to secure your free trial, and embark on your journey through history!

item.title: La Noche Triste: How Aztec Obsidian Defeated Spanish Guns
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
This episode focuses on the Aztec Eagle Warriors, the elite shock troops of the formidable Aztec Empire. Discover their brutal training from infancy, their unique battle tactics focused on capturing enemies for human sacrifice, and their advanced obsidian weaponry. Witness their finest hour during "La Noche Triste," when they handed Hernán Cortés and his Spanish conquistadors a devastating defeat. Explore the historical context, the clash of cultures, and the surprising reasons behind their ultimate demise.
Do you love uncovering the past, exploring historic sites, and venturing to distant lands? Join History Hit today and stream hundreds of exclusive documentaries, with new releases every week. Plus, enjoy ad-free access to our podcast network for even more history every week.

item.title: Harald Hardrada: The Deadliest Viking To Ever Live?
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
This documentary delves into the incredible story of how these Norsemen travelled from the frontiers of Europe to become the personal bodyguards, secret police, and shock troops of the Emperors in Constantinople. Discover their brutal and effective battle tactics, from the impenetrable shield wall to the devastating "bore snout" formation, and witness the lavish, and often debauched, lifestyle they enjoyed in the richest city on Earth.

item.title: The Viking Berserkers Who Took Psychedelics Before Combat
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
The Berserkers were drugged-up Norse warriors who fought their battles in a trance - one bruiser stalling a Saxon army with just an axe.

item.title: Ghost Warriors: The Harii Nightfighters Of Ancient Germany
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
The deadly work of top ancient assassins. The elite Harii tribe of Germania were trained to fight only at night and inflicted the Roman Army's worst ever defeat.
Do you love uncovering the past, exploring historic sites, and venturing to distant lands? Join History Hit today and stream hundreds of exclusive documentaries, with new releases every week. Plus, enjoy ad-free access to our podcast network for even more history every week.

item.title: Sicarii: The Most Feared Assassins Of The Ancient World
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
Discover the untold story of the Sicarii, a ruthless band of Jewish zealots who waged asymmetric warfare against the mighty Roman Empire in 1st Century AD Jerusalem. This documentary unearths their motivations, covert tactics, and their desperate fight for freedom, culminating in the infamous last stand at Masada. Learn how these ancient assassins became Rome's most persistent and feared enemy, leaving a legacy that still resonates today.
Do you love uncovering the past, exploring historic sites, and venturing to distant lands? Join History Hit today and stream hundreds of exclusive documentaries, with new releases every week. Plus, enjoy ad-free access to our podcast network for even more history every week.

item.title: The 300: Uncovering Ancient History's Greatest Last Stand
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
The legendary Battle of Thermopylae in ancient Greece saw 300 Spartan warriors stand against the overwhelming might of the Persian Empire. This documentary reveals the true story of these elite soldiers, from their brutal training and unwavering discipline to their cunning battle tactics like the formidable phalanx. Discover how their incredible sacrifice bought Greece vital time, ultimately leading to the defeat of Xerxes' forces and preserving Greek freedom. Dive into the world of ancient warfare and explore the making of history's most feared fighting machine.
Do you love uncovering the past, exploring historic sites, and venturing to distant lands? Join History Hit today and stream hundreds of exclusive documentaries, with new releases every week. Plus, enjoy ad-free access to our podcast network for even more history every week.

item.title: The Order Of Assassins: The Deadliest Special Forces Of The Medieval World
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
The Order of Assassins were masters of disguise and stealth, wielding psychological warfare as their greatest weapon. Originating in 11th-century Persia, the Assassins, led by Hassan-i Sabbah, targeted prominent figures, including Sunni leaders, employing tactics like stabbing with stilettos and slashing with concealed weapons. The Assassins successfully assassinated key figures, instilling fear and dread across the Middle East.

item.title: History's Most Feared Warriors: What Made The Samurai So Hard To Fight?
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
Its 1703, the Shogun is plunged into controversy when Samurai prince, Asano Naganori, loses his temper and attacks a senior official. This is the story of how Asano's followers, the 47 Ronin, braved Japan and mounted the perfect Black Op.

item.title: Elite Hawaiian Warriors: How Did The Koa Defeat Captain Cook? | Ancient Black Ops
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
The Hawaiian Koa were elite warriors in a land dominated by war. They were grand masters of a martial art called Lua - a way to break every bone in an enemy's body. When Captain Cook lands on Hawaii, the Koa warriors greet him in the worst way.

Visit our partner channel Bloomberg News for global news and insight in an instant.

item.title: Ciné-Guerrillas: Scenes from the Labudović Reels - Algeria’s War of Independence | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Serbian director Mila Turajlic explores the power of cinema as a weapon in the political struggle. Her film "Ciné-Guerrillas” is a portrait of cameraman Stevan Labudović, who documented the war of independence in Algeria on behalf of Tito.
What do the Algerian liberation struggle and Yugoslavia have in common? Stevan Labudović. In 1959, Tito himself sent his favorite cameraman to Algeria. The world needed to witness resistance to French colonial rule, he believed. The images captured by experienced partisan Labudović gave the lie to the propaganda of the occupiers and their western allies. This is a portrait of a man, a mission and a time - a filmic, ideological and personal story.
For three years, until the proclamation of the Democratic Republic of Algeria, Labudović put himself and his camera at the service of those who were fighting for independence.
After finding newsreel footage in the archive of Filmske Novosti ("filmed news”) in Belgrade, Mila Turajlić contacted the now elderly man and set out to tell his story.
Drawing on a wealth of archive material, diary entries by and interviews with Labudović, as well as accounts from contemporary witnesses, Turajlić looks at the promising origins of the alliance that would become the "Non-Aligned Movement.” Still politically topical, this film is a tribute to Labudović.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. Meet intriguing people, travel to distant lands, get a look behind the complexities of daily life and build a deeper understanding of current affairs and global events. item.title: How Science Can Fix the Media Landscape
item.author: StarTalk
item.content:encoded:
Go to https://ground.news/startalk to stay fully informed on the latest Space and Science news. Save 40% off through our link for unlimited access to the Vantage plan this month.
Can we fix the news? On this minisode, Neil deGrasse Tyson and co-host Gary O’Reilly sit down with Harleen Kaur, former space engineer and founder of Ground News, to explore our current media landscape, navigating bias, and fixing the internet.
Why do different outlets report the same event in completely different ways? What role do funding, audience, and ideology play in shaping headlines? Harleen breaks down how Ground News reverse-engineers news coverage across the political spectrum.
We discuss the defunct Fairness Doctrine and how advertisers and conglomerates play a role in our current media landscape. How can readers develop the skills to avoid filter bubbles and echo chambers? Can science be weaponized in media? What happens when a grain of truth is used to validate misinformation? Could AI make things worse or could it be the tool to identify deepfakes and hidden bias?
Neil questions whether we’re headed for the end of credible online news. Could more exposure to opposing views actually shift opinions?
NOTE: StarTalk+ Patrons can listen to this entire episode commercial-free.
Timestamps: 00:00 - Introduction: Harleen Kaur 04:00 - Fixing the Broken Media Landscape 07:20 - The Fairness Doctrine 09:17 - Bias in the News 11:11 - Misinformation 15:14 - Who Funds the News 18:25 - Media Literacy Tips & The Outrage Engine 25:03 - Science in the News 28:21 - AI: Friend or Foe 31:00 - Is the Internet Broken for Good? 33:46 - A Cosmic Perspective
Check out our second channel, @StarTalkPlus
Get the NEW StarTalk book, 'To Infinity and Beyond: A Journey of Cosmic Discovery' on Amazon: https://amzn.to/3PL0NFn

item.title: The Deadliest Animal On Earth May Surprise You | Deadly 60 | BBC Earth Science
item.author: BBC Earth Science
item.content:encoded:
Steve Backshall and his crew take part in an interesting experiment to discover which substance repels mosquitoes most effectively. Is it garlic, eucalyptus, Marmite or DEET?

item.title: An island paradise for digital nomads? | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
The Portuguese island of Madeira, known as the island of eternal spring, is home to Europe’s first digital nomad village. With its pleasant climate and welcoming community, it attracts young freelancers from all over the world. But as this remote-work hotspot grows, rents and the overall cost of living are also rising, pushing locals out of the market.
Find out more in “The first village for digital nomads”. Watch the full documentary on the DW Documentary YouTube channel.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #madeira #digitalnomad ______

item.title: The Rationale for Continuous Delivery • Dave Farley • YOW! 2018
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at YOW! 2018. #GOTOcon #YOW https://yowcon.com
Dave Farley - Continuous Delivery & DevOps Pioneer, Award-winning Author, Founder & Director of Continuous Delivery Ltd. @ModernSoftwareEngineeringYT
RESOURCES https://bsky.app/profile/davefarley77.bsky.social https://www.continuous-delivery.co.uk https://linkedin.com/in/dave-farley-a67927 https://twitter.com/davefarley77 http://www.davefarley.net
ABSTRACT The production of software is a complex, collaborative process that stretches our ability as human beings to cope with its demands. Many people working in #SoftwareDevelopment spend their careers without seeing what good really looks like.
Our history is littered with inefficient processes creating poor quality output, too late to capitalise on the expected business value. How have we got into this state? How do we get past it? What does good really look like?
Continuous Delivery changes the economics of software development for some of the biggest companies in the world, whatever the nature of their software development, find out how and why.
RECOMMENDED BOOKS David Farley • Modern Software Engineering • https://amzn.to/3GI468M Dave Farley • Continuous Delivery Pipelines • https://amzn.to/3rjetdi Dave Farley & Jez Humble • Continuous Delivery • https://amzn.to/3ocIHwd Dave Farley & many more • Software Architecture Metrics • https://amzn.to/3M3XqG5
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://twitter.com/GOTOcon https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #ContinuousDelivery #SoftwareArchitecture #Complexity #SoftwareComplexity #SoftwareEngineering #Programmer #DaveFarley

item.title: The LUCKIEST moment of WW2?
item.author: Imperial War Museums

item.title: Veteran’s Daughter Juggles Her Job While Caring For Her Bed-Bound Father | Caregiving
item.author: PBS
item.content:encoded:
Watch more: https://to.pbs.org/45m7zcL Lynnette Canedy is a female caregiver who gave up her career in LA to move back home to a small town in Kentucky and care for her disabled, veteran father who has dementia, a host of other medical issues and is no longer able to communicate clearly. As she juggles the challenges of full-time caregiving, her full-time job, and finding time for herself, her love for her father gives her the power to persevere.
"Caregiving", a documentary created with executive producer Bradley Cooper highlighting the challenges and triumphs of caregiving in America, will premiere Tuesday, June 24, 2025 at 9 p.m. ET on PBS and on the PBS YouTube Channel. An early streaming premiere will be on wellbeings.org, PBS.org and on the PBS App begins Tuesday, May 27, 2025. For more information about Caregiving, visit https://wellbeings.org/caregiving/
Well Beings is a national wellness campaign of @WETAPBS television, the flagship PBS station in Washington, DC. 📺
This program is made possible by viewers like you. Support your local PBS station: https://www.pbs.org/donate
Subscribe to the PBS channel for more clips: https://www.youtube.com/PBS/
Enjoy full episodes of your favorite PBS shows anytime, anywhere with the free PBS app: https://to.pbs.org/2QbtzhR
FOLLOW US:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/PBS/ X: https://twitter.com/PBS/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/PBS/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@pbs Threads: https://www.threads.net/@pbs
#caregiver #caregiving #kentucky #CaregivingPBS #WellBeings
Support for Caregiving is made possible by Otsuka America Pharmaceutical Inc.; Comfort Keepers; CareScout Holdings, Inc.; Care.com; Evelyn Y. Davis Foundation; Richard King Mellon Foundation; The Arthur Vining Davis Foundations; Ford Foundation; Ralph C. Wilson, Jr. Foundation; NextFifty Initiative (Next50); National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI); PATH Foundation; Care for All with Respect and Equity (CARE) Fund; The John A. Hartford Foundation; and the Corporation for Public Broadcasting.
Caregiving is a production of Lea Pictures and @WETANationalProductions Washington, D.C., in association with Ark Media. For Ark Media, Chris Durrance serves as Director and Senior Producer; Barak Goodman is Series Producer; and Ruth Fertig is Producer. Executive Producers for Lea Pictures are Bradley Cooper and Weston Middleton. Executive Producers for WETA are John F. Wilson and Tom Chiodo. Producer for WETA is Kate Kelly. Production is managed for WETA by Jim Corbley. Consulting Producer is Paul Irving.
National outreach and engagement partners for Caregiving include Rosalynn Carter Institute for Caregivers; Grantmakers In Aging; Milken Institute | Future of Aging; Global Coalition on Aging; Wallis Annenberg GenSpace; Elizabeth Dole Foundation; National Partnership for Healthcare and Hospice Innovation; National Minority Health Association; Fred Rogers Productions; Caregiver Action Network; The Coalition to Transform Advanced Care; American Association of Caregiving Youth; Caring Across Generations; SHRM and SHRM Foundation; National Alliance for Caregiving; Lutheran Services in America; Center to Advance Palliative Care; PEOPLE; and Forbes.com.

Can you still trust the government’s vaccine recommendations?

item.title: How Big Tech Is Quietly Taking Over AI (Without Mergers)
item.author: Patrick Boyle
item.content:encoded:
😎 Get an exclusive 15% discount on Saily data plans! Use code BOYLE at checkout. Download Saily app or go to https://saily.com/boyle
In the race to dominate generative AI, Big Tech firms haven’t just been building, they’ve been buying. But there’s something strange about most of the deals that they have struck. Companies like Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, Google, and Nvidia are embedding themselves deep within the AI ecosystem through strategic investments, exclusive partnerships, and talent acquisitions- with deals that stop just short of formal takeovers, but the economic impact of these deals it turns out – is indistinguishable from full control.
Drayton D'Silva Substack Article: https://enterprisevalue.substack.com/if-i-did-it
Patrick's Books: Statistics For The Trading Floor: https://amzn.to/3eerLA0 Derivatives For The Trading Floor: https://amzn.to/3cjsyPF Corporate Finance: https://amzn.to/3fn3rvC
Ways To Support The Channel Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/PatrickBoyleOnFinance Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/patrickboyle
Visit our website: https://www.onfinance.org Follow Patrick on Twitter Here: https://bsky.app/profile/pboyle.bsky.social
Business Inquiries ➡️ sponsors@onfinance.org
Patrick Boyle On Finance Podcast: Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/7uhrWlDvxzy9hLoW0EYf0b Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/patrick-boyle-on-finance/id1547740313 Google Podcasts: https://tinyurl.com/62862nve
Join this channel to support making this content: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCASM0cgfkJxQ1ICmRilfHLw/join

item.title: How the internet warps our emotions | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Is the internet making us emotionally numb? Online trolls and influencers expertly manipulate people's feelings, leading many to disconnect from their emotions. Scientists explain how the internet influences what we feel — and whether we feel at all.
A man lies in bed, illuminated by the blue-white light of his smartphone screen. As he scrolls through endless social media feeds, he sees adorable pets, outraged opinion pieces, and haunting images from conflict zones - but he feels absolutely nothing.
With curiosity and humor, director David Borenstein travels to Europe, Asia, the U.S., and Russia to investigate how bad things really are. Who is pulling the strings when the internet makes us angry, sad, horny or just plain indifferent? Is there any way to reclaim our emotions? Borenstein portrays a range of perspectives, including an American internet troll, a burnt-out star from the Asian influencer industry, a Russian state propagandist, and an online dominatrix. Scientific research into human emotions sheds light on how our emotional responses are being manipulated. The result is an alarming diagnosis of our digital era — paired with a bold attempt to search for solutions.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #emotions #doomscrolling ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. Meet intriguing people, travel to distant lands, get a look behind the complexities of daily life and build a deeper understanding of current affairs and global events. Subscribe and explore the world around you with DW Documentary.
Subscribe to: ‬ ⮞ DW Documentary (English): https://www.youtube.com/dwdocumentary ⮞ DW Documental (Spanish): https://www.youtube.com/dwdocumental ⮞ DW Documentary وثائقية دي دبليو (Arabic): https://www.youtube.com/dwdocarabia ⮞ DW Documentary हिन्दी (Hindi): https://www.youtube.com/dwdochindi ⮞ DW Doku (German): https://www.youtube.com/dwdoku

item.title: How The U.S. Presidency Has Changed Through History | Compilation
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
In what has become the most powerful, most intense, and most draining job on planet earth, today we are looking at all aspects of being President of the United States. America has had many presidents come and go, and over the years, the job of president has evolved, become more complex, and has even gained perks. Today we will see some of the wild lives presidents lived BEFORE (and AFTER) they were president, some of the weirdest hobbies presidents have had, and we will take a look at such modern marvels as Airforce One and 'The Beast'.
Chapters: 00:00:00 - When A President’s Party Destroys The White House... 00:11:12 - Presidents Who Lived Wild Lives Before Taking Office 00:22:52 - US Presidents with the Strangest Hobbies 00:35:08 - What American Presidents Served at US State Dinners 00:48:05 - How President Taft Tried to Make Billy Possum Happen 00:58:41 - What It Is Like to Ride In the Presidential Limo 01:09:09 - Was Andrew Jackson America's Craziest President? 01:20:02 - What Life Is Like For an Ex-President 01:32:02 - Infamous Presidential Sex Scandals (That Aren’t All Clinton)
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#usa #president #weirdhistory

item.title: Incredible Lusotitan Mating Ritual | Walking with Dinosaurs | BBC Earth Science
item.author: BBC Earth Science
item.content:encoded:
In the prehistoric world, first impressions matter and sometimes a few moves can make all the difference. A male Lusotitan puts his best foot forward to win over a mate, but too much enthusiasm could almost cost him everything.
Best of Earth Science: http://bit.ly/EarthLabOriginals Best of BBC Earth: http://bit.ly/TheBestOfBBCEarthVideos
Taken from 'Walking with Dinosaurs' (2025) Six dig sites. Six stories. Six prehistoric heroes. Discover the incredible lives of dinosaurs unearthed from real-world excavations in a reimagining of the iconic series. Pioneering special effects recreate the twists and turns of the age of the dinosaurs in character-led stories with a truly heavyweight cast.
This is a channel from BBC Studios who help fund new BBC programmes. Service information and feedback: http://bbcworldwide.com/vod-feedback--contact-details.aspx

item.title: Using Generative AI to Strengthen & Accelerate Learning • Barbara Oakley • GOTO 2024
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen 2024. #GOTOcon #GOTOcph https://gotocph.com
Barbara Oakley - Professor of Engineering at Oakland University & Author of Numerous Books @BarbaraOakley
RESOURCES https://twitter.com/Barbara97881398 https://www.linkedin.com/in/barbaraoakley https://barbaraoakley.com
Links https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762 https://psycnet.apa.org/record/2025-07056-001 https://psycnet.apa.org/record/1984-11828-001 https://psycnet.apa.org/record/2016-51296-001 https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29891660 https://psycnet.apa.org/record/2023-71875-001 https://psycnet.apa.org/record/2014-36030-003
ABSTRACT This talk will demonstrate how AI, via transformers, can lift limits on core cognitive processes like attention, synthesis, and retention.
These insights will allow you to use AI more effectively to scaffold and accelerate your own learning.
After all, generative AI can do far more to propel our learning than simply answer questions (important and helpful though that may be!).
Generative AI is, in essence, a teacher—but it can teach better if you know how its properties are sometimes similar to, and sometimes very different from, how the human brain works. [...]
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro 03:03 What does ChatGPT do? 12:32 The value of metaphor 21:35 7 int. companies with their foundational LLMs 26:25 Large language models 34:33 Cheating or "learning lightly" 36:11 The Flynn Effect 39:08 How to think about the new complex world 46:45 Good books on GenAI 48:30 Outro
Download slides and read the full abstract here: https://gotocph.com/2024/sessions/3168
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Barbara Oakley & Olav Schewe • Learn Like a Pro • https://amzn.to/3Z3gyf9 Barbara Oakley, Beth Rogowsky & Terrence J. Sejnowski • Uncommon Sense Teaching • https://amzn.to/4hKGaEW Barbara Oakley & Terrence J. Sejnowski • Learning How to Learn • https://amzn.to/3Cqe7dW Barbara Oakley • A Mind For Numbers • https://amzn.to/3Z2tjqi Barbara Oakley • Mindshift • https://amzn.to/3Z2EAaf Barbara Oakley, Madhavan, Green, Koon & Low • Practicing Sustainability • https://amzn.to/4fn95gZ Barbara Oakley • Pathological Altruism • https://amzn.to/48LDVNK C. Edward Watson & Jose Antonio Bowen • Teaching with AI • https://amzn.to/44sBgYC Terrence J. Sejnowski • ChatGPT and the Future of AI • https://amzn.to/45VnfEf Ethan Mollick • Co-Intelligence • https://amzn.to/3HTnNjK Brian Christian • The Alignment Problem • https://amzn.to/4k5D8LA Dr. Fei-Fei Li • The Worlds I See • https://amzn.to/44b2EJm Kai-Fu Lee • AI Superpowers • https://amzn.to/43YftrN
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://twitter.com/GOTOcon https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #GenAI #ArtificialIntelligence #GenerativeAI #HowToLearn #StrengthenLearning #Mindshift #LearnLikeAPro #ChatGPT #Cloude #LLaMA #DeclarativeLearning #FlynnEffect #UncommonSenseTeaching #TodayInTech #BarbaraOakley

item.title: Better Value Sooner Safer Happier • Simon Rohrer & Eduardo da Silva
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This interview was recorded for the GOTO Book Club. http://gotopia.tech/bookclub
Read the full transcription of the interview here (https://gotopia.tech/episodes/356) Simon Rohrer - Co-Author of "Better Value Sooner Safer Happier" & Senior Director at Saxo Bank Eduardo da Sliva - Independent Consultant on Organization, Architecture, and Leadership Modernization
RESOURCES Simon https://bsky.app/profile/simon.bvssh.com https://mastodon.social/@simonr https://x.com/sirohrer https://www.linkedin.com/in/simonrohrer https://github.com/sirohrer https://www.soonersaferhappier.com
Eduardo https://bsky.app/profile/esilva.net https://mastodon.social/@eduardodasilva https://x.com/emgsilva https://www.linkedin.com/in/emgsilva https://github.com/emgsilva https://esilva.net
DESCRIPTION Eduardo da Silva and Simon Rohrer discuss the core ideas of "Better Value Sooner Safer Happier" diving into the principles of organizational transformation.
Simon shares insights on the shift from output-driven to outcome-focused thinking, emphasizing value over productivity, and the need for continuous improvement in delivery speed, stakeholder satisfaction, and safety. The conversation explores key concepts like technical excellence, integrating safety into development, and balancing incremental changes with occasional larger steps.
Simon Rohrer discusses organizational patterns and the importance of decentralizing decision-making, recommending a flexible, context-driven approach to transformation. The session concludes with practical advice on how to start implementing these ideas, using the book’s map to guide organizations toward the right transformation strategy based on their specific goals.
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Jonathan Smart, Zsolt Berend, Myles Ogilvie & Simon Rohrer • Sooner Safer Happier (https://amzn.to/3Emm9p2) Stephen Fishman & Matt McLarty • Unbundling the Enterprise (https://amzn.to/3W2EcXw) Carliss Y. Baldwin • Design Rules, Vol. 2 (https://amzn.to/4hdllRr) Matthew Skelton & Manuel Pais • Team Topologies Forsgren, Humble & Kim • Accelerate: The Science of Lean Software and DevOps (http://amzn.to/3sVLyLQ) Kim, Humble, Debois, Willis & Forsgren • The DevOps Handbook (https://amzn.to/47oAf3l)

item.title: How a Hong Kong Dynasty Was Hit by China's Property Crisis

item.title: Andrew addresses recent shadow ban
item.author: Channel 5 with Andrew Callaghan

item.title: 3D Scans Reveal How This 'Impossible' Castle Was Really Built
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
Grab your copy of The Book: The Ultimate Guide to Rebuilding Civilization today — and don’t forget to use code TIMELINE10 for 10% off: https://bit.ly/3SuBKGW
Discover more from Hungry Minds: https://www.youtube.com/@UCJ8cLPeByl9yUs9jl5CGevw #thebook, #hungrymindsbooks, #howtorebuildcivilization
The Château de Chambord, the "jewel of the French Renaissance" is an architectural marvel. With over 400 rooms and 77 staircases, it was a colossal undertaking commissioned by King Francis I in the 16th century. We delves into its revolutionary design, believed to be influenced by Leonardo da Vinci, and explore the immense challenges faced by the 1,800 workmen, from building on marshy terrain and transporting 220,000 tons of stone, to constructing the iconic double-helix staircase. We will learn about the staggering costs, the unexpected modifications demanded by the king, and how the château served as a hiding place for priceless artworks during WWII. Five centuries later, modern technology helps uncover its secrets, revealing the genius that made Chambord a timeless masterpiece and a UNESCO World Heritage site.
This channel is part of the History Hit Network. Any queries, please contact owned-enquiries@littledotstudios.com

item.title: Are The Fundamental Constants Finely Tuned? | The Naturalness Problem
item.author: PBS Space Time
item.content:encoded:
Learn More About Anydesk: https://anydesk.com/spacetime
Did God have any choice in creating the world? So asked Albert Einstein. He was being poetic. What he really meant, was whether the universe could have been any other way. Could it have had different laws of physics, driven by different fundamental constants. Or is this one vast and complex universe the inevitable result of an inevitable and unique underlying principle, perhaps expressible as a supremely elegant Theory of Everything. It certainly seems that Einstein thought this should be the case … that God had no choice in whether or how to create the world. It seems like a pretty arm-chair philosophical and perhaps unanswerable question, but the modern “problem” of naturalness may lead to an answer.
Check Out Our Patreon Interview with Looking Glass Universe Here: https://youtu.be/QPuVtvIOYcM
Sign Up on Patreon to get access to the Space Time Discord! https://www.patreon.com/pbsspacetime
Check out the Space Time Merch Store https://www.pbsspacetime.com/shop

Austin, Jr. Diana S Faraz Khan Almog Cohen Daniel Jennings Ben McIntosh David Johnston Michael Barton Isaac Suttell Bleys Goodson Mark Delagasse Mark Daniel Cohen Shane Calimlim Tybie Fitzhugh Eric Kiebler Craig Stonaha Frederic Simon Tonyface Matthew Johnson Michael Purcell John Funai Adrien Molyneux Bradley Jenkins Vlad Shipulin Walter Flinn Thomas Dougherty Zac Sweers Dan Warren Joseph Salomone Cal Stephens

item.title: Could You Survive as a Formula 1 Racing Driver?
item.author: History Hit
item.content:encoded:
Luke Tomes travels to the world's first racing track at Brooklands Museum in Weybridge to find out what it took to be a grand prix racing driver over the last century. Donning a traditional racing suit, hat and goggles, Luke drives a Bugatti Baby, based on the 1926 Type 37 Bugatti race car, down the Brooklands finishing straight, the same track used in the first ever British Grand Prix.
Luke is also driven in a replica of the Lagonda driven at the 1935 Le Mans 24-hour race by Dr Dudley Benjafield, before taking on the Lewis Hamilton McLaren simulator to see if he can cut it as a Formula 1 driver in the 21st century.
2026 marks the centenary of the first British Grand Prix which took place at Brooklands in 1926, and the museum will be having lots of celebrations throughout the year – keep an eye out for upcoming announcements on the Brooklands Museum website soon!
Discover the past on History Hit with ad-free exclusive podcasts and documentaries released weekly presented by world renowned historians Dan Snow, Suzannah Lipscomb, Lucy Worsely, Mary Beard and more. Watch, listen and read history wherever you are, whenever you want it. Available on all devices: Apple TV, Amazon Prime Video, Android TV, Samsung Smart TV, Roku, Xbox, Chromecast, and iOs & Android.
If you'd like to watch more great history content like this head on over to History Hit using the following link https://eu1.hubs.ly/H0gW80Z0 to sign up to our History SVOD service.
#formula1 #vintagecars #motorsport #f1movie

item.title: Is Investing Like Blackjack?
item.author: Bloomberg Originals
item.content:encoded:

Boaz Weinstein, founder of Saba Capital, explains to Sonali Basak how the two worlds mirror each other.

Bloomberg Originals offers bold takes for curious minds on today’s biggest topics. Hosted by experts covering stories you haven’t seen and viewpoints you haven’t heard, you’ll discover cinematic, data-led shows that investigate the intersection of business and culture. Exploring every angle of climate change, technology, finance, sports and beyond, Bloomberg Originals is business as you’ve never seen it.
Subscribe for business news, but not as you've known it: exclusive interviews, fascinating profiles, data-driven analysis, and the latest in tech innovation from around the world.
Visit our partner channel Bloomberg News for global news and insight in an instant.

item.title: An endless war: Iran, Israel and the United States (2/2) [Reupload] | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Part 1: https://youtu.be/F1x5psFk9io [This documentary was originally released in 2020]
Since the Islamic Republic of Iran was founded in 1979, there has been a war between Iran, Israel and the US. The war has never been officially declared, and there has been no real reconciliation.
Part One of this two-part documentary focuses on the origins of the conflict between Iran and Israel. It began when the Israelis invaded Lebanon to confront a new Lebanese Shiite force backed by Iran: Hezbollah.
Throughout the Middle East, targeted assassinations, bombings, and non-military attacks with no clear attribution are on the rise. Could the "secret war" between Iran and Israel escalate into a major regional or even global confrontation? The two countries are dominant powers in today’s Middle East. Yet Iran and Israel have failed to resolve their historical disputes, and there has never been significant dialogue between them. And what role does the United States play in the conflict?
Based on painstaking archival research and featuring high-profile players in Israel, Iran, Lebanon and the US, the film looks back on the more than forty years since Iran’s Islamic Revolution. It explores key moments and provides ways of understanding the new Middle East.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #war #iran #usa #isreal ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. Meet intriguing people, travel to distant lands, get a look behind the complexities of daily life and build a deeper understanding of current affairs and global events. Subscribe and explore the world around you with DW Documentary.
Subscribe to: ‬ ⮞ DW Documentary (English): https://www.youtube.com/dwdocumentary ⮞ DW Documental (Spanish): https://www.youtube.com/dwdocumental ⮞ DW Documentary وثائقية دي دبليو (Arabic): https://www.youtube.com/dwdocarabia ⮞ DW Documentary हिन्दी (Hindi): https://www.youtube.com/dwdochindi ⮞ DW Doku (German): https://www.youtube.com/dwdoku
For more visit: http://www.dw.com/en/tv/docfilm/s-3610 Follow DW Documentary on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwdocumentary/ Follow DW Documental on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/dwdocumental

item.title: A tiny dinosaur flapped its wings and made these footprints
item.author: PBS Eons
item.content:encoded:
Follow us over to the PBS App or PBS.org to watch Walking with Dinosaurs! https://to.pbs.org/WalkingWithDinosaurs
***** PBS Member Stations rely on viewers like you. To support your local station, go to http://to.pbs.org/DonateEons *****
Eons is a production of Complexly for PBS Digital Studios.
Super special thanks to the following Patreon patrons for helping make Eons possible: Ashley Glenn, Annemiek Arkema, Willie, Irene Wood, lyric1981, Jason Rostoker, Mary Tevington, Jonathan Rust, IAmHere, Wookiee Henderson, Beth-Ann Cheney, CalamityBangs, Nquiztor, Mark Talbott-Williams, Brian Clubb, SKS PHD, Nomi Alchin, Steve Hill, Duane Westhoff, Hillary Ryde-Collins, Amanda Putz, Eric Edwards, Katherine Smith, Yu Mei, A.B. Heckert, raus , Jennifer Courtemanche, Nick Ryhajlo, Eric Franklin, Dan Caffee, Albert Folsom, Sarah Grunow-Mau, Steve, Walter Ray-Dulany, Aaditya Mehta, Deanna Hernandez, John Celio, Ruth Orr, Stephen Patterson, AllPizzasArePersonal, Randall George, Christopher Samuel, Steven Kern, Lianne Lairmore, Travis Bauer, Collin Dutrow, Alex Hackman, Karen Farrell, Kevin Lacson, John H. Austin, Jr., Gizmo, Betsy Radley, Jeff Graham, Nathan Paskett
If you'd like to support the channel, head over to http://patreon.com/eons and pledge for some cool rewards!
Want to follow Eons elsewhere on the internet? Facebook - https://www.facebook.com/eonsshow Instagram - https://www.instagram.com/eonsshow/ Bluesky - https://bsky.app/profile/pbseons.bsky.social #Eons
References:

item.title: How the Universe Might End
item.author: Kurzgesagt – In a Nutshell
item.content:encoded:
Right now, the universe is full of life and light. But over unimaginable spans of time, everything we know will end. Galaxies will fade, stars will die, and even black holes will disappear, leaving behind a cold, dark void. This is the timeline of the universe's death.
#kurzgesagt #inanutshell #kurzgesagt_inanutshell #learnwithshorts #science #Cosmology #HeatDeath #SpaceScience #Astrophysics
Sources & further reading: https://sites.google.com/view/kgs-tiktok-sources
Follow us for more sciencey content! 🦆

item.title: How Ray Dalio Identifies Top Talent
item.author: Bloomberg Originals
item.content:encoded:
How does Ray Dalio identify top talent?
He breaks down his method for Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly.
Watch the full episode of The Deal on the Bloomberg app or listen wherever you get your podcasts.

Bloomberg Originals offers bold takes for curious minds on today’s biggest topics. Hosted by experts covering stories you haven’t seen and viewpoints you haven’t heard, you’ll discover cinematic, data-led shows that investigate the intersection of business and culture. Exploring every angle of climate change, technology, finance, sports and beyond, Bloomberg Originals is business as you’ve never seen it.
Subscribe for business news, but not as you've known it: exclusive interviews, fascinating profiles, data-driven analysis, and the latest in tech innovation from around the world.

item.title: The Electoral College Explained
item.author: PBS
item.content:encoded:
Ben Sheehan unpacks the Electoral College's intricate complicated history and current complexities in this episode of “Civics Made Easy.” From its founding in 1787 to the modern debates surrounding it, Ben explains why it was created, how it operates today, and the potential for reform. This episode offers an engaging and accessible exploration of one of the most misunderstood parts of our electoral system.
Civics Made Easy | The Electoral College Explained
This program is made possible by viewers like you. Support your local PBS station: https://www.pbs.org/donate
Subscribe to the PBS channel for more clips: https://www.youtube.com/PBS/
Enjoy full episodes of your favorite PBS shows anytime, anywhere with the free PBS app: https://to.pbs.org/2QbtzhR
FOLLOW US:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/PBS/ X: https://twitter.com/PBS/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/PBS/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@pbs Threads: https://www.threads.net/@pbs
#civics #election #voting
Civics Made Easy Hosted by Ben Sheehan, breaks down the complex world of American government and civic participation. Each episode transforms intricate concepts into clear, digestible lessons, equipping viewers with the essential knowledge to actively participate in their democracy. Designed to empower and inform, the series makes understanding civics both accessible and engaging for all.

Since the Islamic Republic of Iran was founded in 1979, there has been a war between Iran, Israel and the US. The war has never been officially declared, and there has been no real reconciliation.
Part One of this two-part documentary focuses on the origins of the conflict between Iran and Israel. It began when the Israelis invaded Lebanon to confront a new Lebanese Shiite force backed by Iran: Hezbollah.
Throughout the Middle East, targeted assassinations, bombings, and non-military attacks with no clear attribution are on the rise. Could the "secret war" between Iran and Israel escalate into a major regional or even global confrontation? The two countries are dominant powers in today’s Middle East. Yet Iran and Israel have failed to resolve their historical disputes, and there has never been significant dialogue between them. And what role does the United States play in the conflict?
Based on painstaking archival research and featuring high-profile players in Israel, Iran, Lebanon and the US, the film looks back on the more than forty years since Iran’s Islamic Revolution. It explores key moments and provides ways of understanding the new Middle East.

item.title: The Dark Truth about US Copper Mining: 5CAST w/ Andrew Callaghan (#8) ft. Naelyn Pike
item.author: Channel 5 with Andrew Callaghan
item.content:encoded:
Use my code channel5 at https://incogni.com/channel5 to get an exclusive 60% off your subscription. New Feature Alert! Incogni now offers Unlimited and Family Unlimited plans — giving you even more protection. With the Custom Removals feature, you can point to any people search site or shady site exposing your info, and Incogni’s team of privacy agents will work to get your data taken down for you. Take back your privacy today.
